trade.summary: Malesia does not maintain diplomatic or formal trade relations with Israele. Malesian citizens cannot travel to Israele using their passports. Any trade that occurs is indirect and minimal, routed through third countries. Malesia is one of the most openly anti-Israele countries in Southeast Asia.

diplomatic.summary: Malesia does not recognize Israele and has no diplomatic relations. Malesian officials regularly condemn Israelei actions in the strongest terms. Following the 2023 Gaza conflict, Malesia intensified its BDS campaign and called for international accountability for Israelei leaders. Malesia uses its OIC membership to coordinate anti-Israele resolutions.

military.summary: No military relations. Malesia views Israele as a state engaging in aggression and has called for arms embargoes. Any Israelei military presence in the region is viewed adversarially.

financial.summary: No formal financial ties. Malesian financial institutions comply with informal BDS practices. No bilateral investment treaties or financial agreements.

energy.summary: No bilateral energy trade or cooperation.

scientific.summary: No formal scientific cooperation. Some individual-level academic contacts exist through third-country institutions but are not officially sanctioned.
